stanley E. Steele

13555 Plantation way
Moreno ValIey, CA 9236o
(7 L4) 242-19 44
March 3O, 199O
JilI stecker, Legaf Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 ttJtt Street, Ste. 600
Sacramento, CA 95814
RE: Request for Formal written Advice from
Stanley E. steefe, Planning Commissioner,
city of Moreno Va11ey
Dear l.{s . Stecker:
I am currently an appointed Pl-annj-ng Cornmissioner for the
city of Moreno Valley. I am writj-ng to you or to whomever
this matter may be assigned, to request your written advice
regarding ny participation in a proposed project which will- be
coming before the Planning Cornmission for public hearing,
currently scheduled for May 3, L99o.
I encfose with this fetter a nap showing the location of
my resj-dence, and al,so showing the boundaries of the project
in rel-ationship to the location of my residence. You will
note from the map the residence is located about 900 feet from
the boundary of the project closest to ny property. 
My
property is or,rned j-n f ee by rnysel f and ny wi f e .
I have reviewed the provisions of Section 7a7o2.3 of the
Fair Pofitical Practj-ce Commissj-on Regulatj-ons, dealing with
ownership interest in real- property indirectly involved in the
decision, and whether i.t constitutes a materiaL financiat
effect, and am uncertaj-n whether I will be barred from voting,
and seek your opinion and any further guidance in thj-s regard
as you may wish to offer.
The proposed project is entitled the rrMoreno Hightands
Specific Planrr which is described in the Executive Summary of
the Draft Specific PIan as a new 3r038-acre master pl-anned
community situated in the eastern area of the city of }loreno
Va11ey, located entirely within the City,s sphere of
influence, with approxirnately 1,800 acres focated within the
boundaries of the City, and the balance Iocated outside of the
City boundarj.es in the County. The area of the proposed
project is largely unimproved land, and the developer is


JiIl Stecker, Legal Division
Fai.r Political- Practices Commission
March 3O, 1990
Page 2
proposing to provide approximately 554 acres of pLanned
business center and approximately 8,458 residential dwellings
"linked together through a comprehensive network of parks,
greenbel-ts, gol-f course and open space. rr The proposed
development is comparable in size to certain other nearby
planned communities in the City, and which have previously
been approved. The difference in the proposed project is that
it is proposed to contain a significant business center and a
major conmunity park. I understand that part of the proposal
includes, in addition to the specific plan, a proposed
annexation and development agreenent, and the underlining
zoning of the area will be changed to a specific planned zone
designed for the project. 
I also enclose with this letter 
a
copy of an illustrative 
plan and Iand use pfan included in the
applicant's summary of the project, and which shows the land
uses general,l,y proposed and the street layout, which, when
compared to the enclosed map showing my residence, will more
fu1]y provide you with the layout of the project as it will be
situated in the general area where my residence is located.
Afso because the project will- differ from what was
generally proposed in the area by the city's general pfan, a
general plan amendment is being processed for the applicant,
as we1l. The enc]osed Executive Summary of the project,
together r^rith a portion of the j-ntroductory summary and
project description, including surnmary of irnpacts, from the
draft environmentaf impact report submitted with the project,
more fuffy describes the project, and includes project
impacts, including the fact that the proposed project would
renove most of the present (RuraI Residential) desj-gnation and
a najority of the R1 designation (one unit to the acre) ,
double the area of R2 (2 units per acre) , increase the area of
R3 (3 units per acre) more than 20 times, decrease the P1 area
by 854, introduce more dense residentiaf uses and neh,
commercial, business park, public and open space land use
categories. In effect, the project would be nore honogeneous
or consistent with other areas of the City, and would preclude
the retention of a rural eastern end (where the project is
located) and wouLd precl-ude a more intense industrial
development of the portion of the site designated as P1 in the
current genera1 p1an.
My current residence is located on a re]atively large lot
(approxirnately l/2 acre), fronting on a smaff cul--de-sac
street as shown on the enclosed rnap. My wi-fe and I reside in
our home, and it is not rented out and we do not anticipate
renting it in the foreseeable future. 
Since our lot and the


JiIl 
Steckerr Legal Oivision
Fair Political 
Practices Commission
March 30, 199O
Page 3
adjacent residential lots have been subdivided as a large l-ot
development, it would not appear that the project will have an
effect on the current use or r^rould LikeLy result i"n a change
of use. The character of our irnrnediate neighborhood is set
and would not likely be changed, al,though obviousl-y the
character of the project site, itseff, will change
substantially. 
Since we are on a cuI-de-sac, the effect of
traffic 
on our street wouLd not likely be significant. 
I
don't bel-ieve the noise fevels rrril-l- significantly increase on
our lot, and I do not anticipate a negative or positive effect
v/ith respect to view, privacy or aj-r ornissions as these iterns
affect our property.
I have not hired an independent real- estate appraiser to
appraise our property (before and after the project approval,
if approved), and do not anticipate going through the expense
of doing so, and the city does not have a policy of providing
appraisers under such circurnstances. Land values generally
have been increasing and I anticipate wilI continue to
increase in the city, and aLthough I am not absolutely
positive, I am not ar^rare that the project (whether it is
approved or not approved) will affect the value of my property
(either up or down) by $10,o00 or more, not considering the
general infl"ationary property valuation trends in the City.
I believe if I were to place rny residence on the market
today, it would tikely seII ior u pii"" between $200,000 and'
$250,000.
I r,rill be happy to provide you with any further
information you feel is necessary. I can be reached at the
above phone number if anything further is needed, and I would
sincerely appreciate heari-ng from you prior to the
commencement of the hearings on this project.
I hope I have provided you with enough information to
give me specific advice concerning l,rhether or not I should
parti-cipate in the project. 
As a supplement to that, I would
appreciate receiving any specific suggestions or guidance you
may be able to give me to assist in determining both for this
project and other projects which may come up, whether they


Jil1 Stecker, Legal Division
Fair Pol-itical Practices Commission
l.tarch 30, 1990
Page 4
$rouLd have a material financial, effect such as to preclude my
participation.
Thank you kindly for your consideration.
A.YI\|225

sections.question_synthetic: A Planning Commissioner for the City of Moreno Valley seeks formal written advice on whether his property ownership approximately 900 feet from a proposed 3,038-acre master-planned community project constitutes a material financial effect that would bar his participation in the Planning Commission's review of the project under FPPC Regulations Section 7a702.3.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. Document contains no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections in the formal sense - rather, it is the applicant's letter requesting advice. The question_synthetic was synthesized from the content of the request. Document includes extensive enclosures (map, project plans, executive summary) referenced but not included in the text provided.

embedding.summary: Stanley E. Steele, an appointed Planning Commissioner for the City of Moreno Valley, requests formal written advice regarding whether his participation in reviewing the proposed Moreno Highlands Specific Plan would create a conflict of interest, given that his residence is located approximately 900 feet from the project boundary. He provides detailed information about the project scope, his property characteristics, and his analysis of potential impacts to his property value.
